candidates.why: Represents top-line gross revenue; numeric values match Total Revenue, indicating it is a primary revenue label.
Explicit total revenue line with same figures as Gross Revenue; best single top-line revenue label and should be primary priority.
Finance income, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Represents net income available to common shareholders, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Share of comprehensive income on investments accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: Equity-method income, not revenue.
Includes net income plus OCI, not revenue.
Balance sheet asset, not a revenue flow item.

variables.notes: Total Revenue is the clearest single top-line revenue label; Gross Revenue is included as a fallback in case of label changes over time.

candidates.why: Direct shipping/voyage operating costs; appropriate component of cost of goods sold for a shipping company.
Direct vessel chartering costs; another core component of cost of sales.
Represents ship operating expenses, likely direct operating costs rather than overhead; suitable as COGS in this industry context.
Explicitly labeled labor costs in COGS; should be included as a COGS component even if preview values are currently nan.
Operating expenses related to leases that are part of operating cost base; best treated as part of COGS rather than SG&A.
Corporate overhead SG&A, not direct production/service costs; should be excluded from COGS.
Financial/hedging costs rather than direct operating costs; normally excluded from COGS.
Labor expenses specifically within SG&A; overhead, not COGS.
Generic labor overhead line, not clearly tied to production COGS; better treated as SG&A if used at all.
Appears under G&A section; more consistent with SG&A than COGS.
Likely other operating/overhead expenses, not clearly direct COGS.
Net other operating items, mix of income/expense; not a pure COGS line.
Could be overhead or operating; insufficient evidence it is core COGS here.
Generic staff overhead, not clearly COGS-specific in this layout.
Balance sheet liability, not a P&L COGS component.

variables.notes: Selected rows represent the main direct operating cost components for a shipping company. SG&A-type and financial/hedging lines were excluded.

candidates.why: In this industry context it appears to be ship operating costs (more like COGS) rather than corporate SG&A, so not ideal for XSGA.
Lease-related operating costs tied to operations; more akin to COGS than SG&A.
Explicit G&A overhead line with non-empty values; per hierarchy, this should be used as the SG&A measure.
Specific SG&A labor component, but appears empty in preview; can be implicitly covered by the main G&A bucket.
Explicit SG&A label but appears all nan in preview; less reliable than the populated G&A line here.
Additional G&A component, but with nan values in the preview; secondary to the main G&A line.
Personnel costs likely part of SG&A, but shown as a subcomponent under G&A; the explicit G&A aggregate is preferred per rules.
Mixed income/expense, not pure SG&A; should generally be excluded from XSGA components.

variables.notes: An explicit, populated G&A line exists; per SG&A hierarchy, only that aggregate is used for XSGA_COMPONENTS, rather than mixing in COGS-like operating expenses or minor subcomponents.
No row labels referencing research or development were found in the provided Income Statement or Balance Sheet, so R&D expense appears not to be reported separately.

candidates.why: Primary aggregate interest expense line; best main candidate for XINT (most negative values, capturing total interest).
Component of interest expense excluding leases; good secondary label if the main line is missing in some years.
Lease-related portion of interest expense under IFRS 16; tertiary fallback label for XINT.
Represents non-controlling interests, not interest expense.
Non-controlling interests, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest; valid as a lower-priority interest expense label.
Balance sheet debt principal, not an interest expense flow.
Same as above; liability, not expense.
Debt balance, not expense.
Long-term debt balance, not expense.
Long-term debt balance, not expense.
Equity for non-controlling interests, not an interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: XINT is mapped to the primary interest expense line with additional specific interest components as fallbacks; balance sheet debt amounts and minority interest lines are excluded.

candidates.row_label: Share of comprehensive income on investments accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: Income statement item related to associates, not an equity balance.
Component of equity, not total book equity.
Minor adjustment line, not total equity.
Single-year equity figure, likely similar to total equity, but less complete time series than Total Equity.
Explicit total equity line with full time series; best proxy for book equity given no separate parent/owner equity line.
Duplicate style of the total equity line; same numeric values, may apply to other periods/formats.

variables.notes: No explicit ‘Equity attributable to owners/parent’ line exists. Total Equity is therefore used as book equity, with the stylistic variant Total equity as a fallback label. Per instructions, MIB is set to empty to avoid double counting any non-controlling interests that might be embedded in Total Equity.

candidates.why: P&L allocation to non-controlling interests, not the balance sheet minority equity balance required for MIB.
Same as above; income allocation, not the balance sheet item.
Balance sheet minority interest line, but appears to be zero across preview years; however, since BE is based on Total Equity, including this risks double counting if it were non-zero.

variables.notes: Book equity (BE) is mapped to Total Equity, which likely already includes any non-controlling interests. To avoid potential double counting per instructions, MIB is set to an empty list, effectively treating minority interests as zero in the PROF computation.
